narratives.evidence_quote: दुनिया बारूद के ढेर पर बैठी है.
narratives.reasoning: This phrase directly evokes fear and anxiety by portraying the world as sitting on a 'heap of gunpowder', suggesting an imminent and widespread threat of conflict.

narratives.evidence_quote: उससे लग रहा है कि इजरायल, मिडिल ईस्ट का नया बादशाह बन सकता है.
narratives.reasoning: This statement speculates on a potential future outcome of the conflict, predicting that Israel could become the 'new king' of the Middle East.

subnarratives.subnarrative_name: URW: Amplifying war-related fears: Russia will also attack other countries
subnarratives.evidence_quote: नाटो की फौजें रूस को चुनौती दे रही हैं.
subnarratives.reasoning: This statement frames NATO forces as actively challenging Russia, implying a direct military confrontation is underway, which supports the subnarrative that Russia's conflict is expanding and it may attack other countries (NATO members).

subnarratives.subnarrative_name: URW: Amplifying war-related fears: NATO should/will directly intervene
subnarratives.evidence_quote: नाटो की फौजें रूस को चुनौती दे रही हैं.
subnarratives.reasoning: The phrase directly states that NATO forces are challenging Russia, which is an active promotion of the idea that NATO is or will be directly involved in military action against Russia.

subnarratives.evidence_quote: दुनिया जंग के ढेर पर बैठी है... कुल मिलाकर दुनिया बारूद के ढेर पर बैठी है.
subnarratives.reasoning: This repeated metaphor of the world sitting on a heap of war/explosives amplifies general, catastrophic war-related fear, supporting the parent narrative but not fitting the specific definitions of nuclear war, WW3, or Russia attacking others/NATO intervention as defined.
